rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LENA MAE RENFRO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/03/2024 (no earlier date available). 1) An original small estate affidavit signed by each successor of Esma Renfro (who survived the decedent and whose interest in this estate vested) must be filed with a certified copy of the death certificate. This document CANNOT be e-filed and must be filed conventionally with a pleading cover sheet attached. 2) An original small estate affidavit signed by each successor of James Renfro (who survived the decedent and whose interest in this estate vested) must be filed with a certified copy of the death certificate. This document CANNOT be e-filed and must be filed conventionally with a pleading cover sheet attached. 3) An original small estate affidavit signed by each successor of Belinda Renfro (who survived the decedent and James Renfro and whose interest in this estate vested) must be filed with a certified copy of the death certificate. This document CANNOT be e-filed and must be filed conventionally with a pleading cover sheet attached. Note: Belinda Renfro's successors must sign two affidavits: one for the estate of James Renfro; and one for the estate of Belinda Renfro. See Probate Code sections 11801(a), 11802(c), and 13100 et seq; see also LRSF 14.35(G) & 14.59(C)(2)(6). If a small estate affidavit cannot be obtained from all the successors (or cannot be used for any other reason), distribution must be made under section 11802(a). A certified copy of letters issued to the personal representative within 60 days before the hearing must be conventionally filed with a pleading cover sheet attached (CANNOT be e-filed), or a certification of authority for summary administration issued by a public administrator under section 7660(a)(2)(A) must be filed if distribution is made to the public administrator acting as personal representative to proceed with summary disposition.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF JESUS PULE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B. Continued to 2/14/24. No proposed order. No proof of service. No bank statements. No additional accounting following the date of death. The petition appears to be incomplete (no Exhibit A) and includes prayers that do not make sense or appear contradictory. Did the conservatee die testate or intestate? Has an estate been opened yet for him? Did he have a trust? Deadline for providing the assigned examiner a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues is 2/2/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TONI MARIE MOORE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/3/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Paragraph 8 of the amendment filed on 11/17/23 shows an incorrect total fee base. A second amendment, signed and verified by the administrator, must be filed to correct the amount and proposed distribution. Courtesy copies of the administrator's second amendment and proof of service of notice of that second amendment must be received by the assigned Examiner by 12/20/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (It is unclear why the amendment did not simply address the questions about the tangible personal property and why another attorney declaration, albeit verified by the administrator this time, was filed to "respond" to issues. No Examiner Notes were sent; issues were posted in the tentative ruling.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DAVID K BALL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/17/2024, as nothing has been filed to addess the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 10/27/2023): 1) No additional inventory and appraisal has been filed since the partial one only listing small cash was filed in March 2023. 2) Has the petitioner taken possession of all the securities appraised by the referee in June 2022? If not, please describe efforts the petitioner has made and other updates since her supplemental declaration filed in February 2023. 3) Please provide an itemized list of assets escheated to the state and sought to be recovered. 4) The decedent died more than 2 years ago, but no interim account has been filed. If the sole beneficiary has waived the first account, please provide a copy of her signed waiver. 5) Has the petitioner taken any IAEA actions reportable under CRC 7.250(a)? 6) Each status report must set forth all the information required by LRSF 14.35(J)(1), eve if it was included in previous reports. Allegations re notice under Probate Code section 9202 and assignments or powers of attorney that may affect distribution must be provided.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ELIAS S. WEINTRAUB
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Granted per written order (A proposed order, whether revised or otherwise, is just a proposed order until it is signed. When papers are not filed electronically, each paper presented for filing must contain two prepunched normal-sized holes, centered 2-1/2 inches apart and 5/8 inch from the top of the paper. [CRC 2.115.] This includes proposed orders.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALETTA MARIE LURIE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/29/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proof of service has been filed. (2) Notice must also include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (3) Detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sale must be filed to support the statutory fee. [CRC 7.705(b) and 7.550(b)(6).] (4) Administrator alleges in paragraph 4 of the petition that he sold all of the securities, but then alleges in paragraph 18 that he has $203,474.63 in "cash, equities, and mutual funds" as of 9/1/23. (5) Then, in paragraph 38, he claims assets on hand total $201,167.48 without any explanation for the difference. (6) Is the administrator NOT requesting at $525 reserve, as mentioned in paragraph 31? The prayer requests authority to distribute funds remaining from the $500 reserve... (7) No proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (8) No courtesy copies have been received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] Petition was reviewed online as a courtesy. Please provide a proposed order and a courtesy copy of the amendment to the petition to the assigned examiner by 1/22/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The time for filing creditor claims expired on 5/23/23, four months after Letters were issued, not 3/12/22. The 13100 affidavit has not yet been reviewed.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE MATILDA CYTRON TRUST U/D/T DATED MARCH 20, 1978, as amended
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition 1 For Order Determining Questions Of Interpretation Of Trust Probate Code 17200b1 And Instructing Trustee To Follow Terms Of Trust 2) For Order Compelling Trustee To Account In Accordance With Probate Code Sections 1060-1064; 3) For Redress Of Breach Of Trust 4) For Order Removing Trustee; 5) Order Allowing Attorney Feess And Coscts
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/03/2024, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (see tentative ruling for 10/25/2023 hearing): 1) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form at least 30 days before the hearing. 2) The proofs of service attached to the DE-120 forms did not list Leonard Horowitz. 3) The petition caption and item 1 of the DE-120 forms did not refer to an order directing Leonard to distribute Lenora's share as additional relief sought. CRC 7.50 & 7.102. 4) Paragraph 8 of the petition: i) The addresses of all beneficiaries and trustees (other than the petitioner and Sally Gati) must be stated. Probate Code section 17201 & CRC 7.902. ii) Daniel Cytron is not listed. iii) David Gati is not listed. iv) Is Chris Lenz a beneficiary? If so, his address must be stated. 5) Lenora's ownership interest in the Benedict Canyon Property at the time of her death is alleged to be 48.74%, but according to Exhibit C (attorney's letter to Sally Gati), the percentage is 42.685%. 6) Please specify the percentage of Shirley's ownership interest in the Benedict Canyon Property at the time of her death. 7) Is the petitioner's claim for breach of trust timely under section 16460(a)(2)?

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PIK KAU LEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/14/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been submitted or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No courtesy copies have been received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] (3) No proof of service of notice has been filed. (Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.]) (4) No proof of publication of the notice has been filed. (5) No Receipt of Acknowledgment of the Duties and Liabilities has been filed. (6) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (7) Waiver of bond by all heirs does not appear to be attached to the petition or filed separately. (8) If a decedent is survived by issue, items 6 and 7 of the petition should not be completed. Please provide a hard copy proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 2/2/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
